diagrams.description: The mind map of labor economics branches out from the central concept into five key areas. Labor supply covers participation, hours, human capital, and migration. Labor demand focuses on marginal productivity, derived demand, and elasticity. Wage determination explores competitive equilibrium, monopsony, efficiency wages, and collective bargaining. Institutions include trade unions, minimum wage laws, unemployment insurance, and employment protection legislation. Finally, outcomes encompass employment, unemployment, wage inequality, and discrimination. This structure provides a comprehensive overview of the field’s main components.
